a11y: Renders as <a> — inherently keyboard and screen-reader accessible.
Use link for navigation; use button for actions (do not style a <button> as a link for navigation).
External links: add aria-label or visually-hidden text to warn users ("opens in new tab").
boundaries: link.tsx exports both Link (variant + size) and LinkButton — the kit's replacement for shadcn's Button variant=link.
description: A text hyperlink (the kit's answer to shadcn variant=link, since Button has none); inline navigation in text. variant + size.
name: link
notes: hover/focus/active states are CSS pseudo-classes, not props; disabled is the only state prop.
rationale: Mirrors two Figma sets on the Link page: • `link` (372:4599): an inline text link. State axis (default/hover/active/ visited/focused/disabled) → CSS; `destructive` is a real variant. • `link-button` (492:2073): a text+icon button styled as a link, Size lg/md/sm/xs. Colors bind the `--ds-color-content-link*` semantic tokens (blue ramp).
title: Link

do: Use link for navigation and button for actions — never a styled <button> for navigation.
Use LinkButton (same file) for navigation that should look like a button.
Warn on external links via aria-label or visually-hidden text ("opens in new tab").
dont: Don't reach for a Button variant="link" — Button has no link variant in this kit; this component is the answer.

whenToUseInstead.when: the element performs an action instead of navigating
